risk_tasks.task: Ingest all upstream outputs. Confirm you have received: Extractor output (structured extraction) Financial Analyst output (deal economics analysis) Rights & Clearances Analyst output (rights scope and clearance analysis) Talent & Guild Compliance Analyst output (talent protections and guild compliance) Regulatory & Compliance Analyst output (regulatory obligations and gaps) If any specialist output is missing, note this and proceed with available inputs. Flag the gap in your output.

Build the cross-reference index: collect all cross_reference tags from specialist outputs. These are issues that one specialist flagged for another. Verify that each cross-referenced issue was addressed by the target agent. If not, analyze the cross-referenced issue yourself and include it in findings.

Tag all key and defined terms: Start with every defined term from the Extractor's defined_terms section Add significant undefined-but-operative terms identified during review (e.g., "broadcast-quality", "meaningful consultation", "reasonable efforts") For each term, assign: category (FINANCIAL, RIGHTS_GRANT, TEMPORAL, PARTY, TERRITORY, PLATFORM_MEDIA, DELIVERY, LEGAL_PROTECTION, CONTENT_STANDARD, COMPENSATION, TERMINATION, EXCLUSIVITY, REPORTING, OTHER), significance (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW based on this contract's context), and a contextual note explaining why the term matters in this specific deal

Identify vague or ill-fitting terms: Review the Extractor's defined terms for circular definitions, undefined references, or internal inconsistency Review Financial Analyst's ambiguities for terms that prevent payment computation Review Rights Analyst's silent zone items for terms with ambiguous scope Review Talent Analyst's findings for provisions with vague trigger definitions (morals clause, key person) Review Regulatory Analyst's gaps for undefined compliance responsibilities Merge into a unified list. For each: state the exact term, where it appears, why it is vague, and what the practical impact of the vagueness is

Identify red flags: Review Financial Analyst's risks for provisions that are unusually one-sided or deviate from industry norms Review Rights Analyst's gaps for deficient chain of title, missing clearances, or inadequate holdbacks Review Talent Analyst's compliance issues for CBA violations and protection deficiencies Review Regulatory Analyst's high-severity gaps for unaddressed regulatory obligations with enforcement consequences For each red flag: state the provision, severity (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW), the concern, and the industry norm as a benchmark

Identify unclear provisions: Review all specialist outputs for provisions that are present but operatively ambiguous — missing details, dangling cross-references, contradictory language Check for cross-provision inconsistencies: does a term used in Article 4 mean something different from how it is defined in Article 1? For each: state what the provision says, what is ambiguous, and what clarification would resolve it

Identify missing standard provisions: Determine the contract type from the Extractor's metadata Compare the extracted provisions against the expected provisions for this contract type per foundation_contract_knowledge Cross-check against specialist findings: Financial Analyst's missing payment mechanics, Rights Analyst's unaddressed rights areas, Talent Analyst's protection gaps, Regulatory Analyst's regulatory gaps For each missing provision: state what is missing, why it is expected for this contract type, and the risk of omission Do NOT flag provisions as missing if they are irrelevant to this contract type

Identify asymmetric obligations: Review Financial Analyst's party-perspective analysis for economic imbalances Review Rights Analyst's findings for one-sided clearance warranties or holdback obligations Review Talent Analyst's one-sided provisions Distinguish inherent role-based asymmetry (not a finding) from drafted asymmetry that exceeds what the party roles require (a finding) For each: state the imbalance, which party benefits, and why it exceeds normal role-based asymmetry

Produce ranked negotiation priorities: Collect all findings from tasks 4-8 plus all risks, gaps, and issues from specialist outputs Deduplicate: merge findings that address the same underlying issue from different specialist perspectives Assign each item to a tier: MUST_RESOLVE, SHOULD_RESOLVE, or NICE_TO_HAVE Within each tier, rank by: (1) economic impact, (2) exploitation-blocking potential, (3) litigation/enforcement probability, (4) regulatory consequence severity Limit MUST_RESOLVE to no more than 10 items. If more exist, the contract may be fundamentally deficient — note this. For each priority: state the item, its tier, the rationale, and trace it back to the specific specialist finding(s) that support it

Determine overall risk posture: Count findings by severity across all categories Assess the distribution of priority tiers Consider the specialist agents' overall posture assessments: Financial (deal structure), Rights (clean/conditional/deficient), Talent (strong/adequate/weak/deficient), Regulatory (compliant/partial/deficient) Assign: LOW, MODERATE, HIGH, or CRITICAL with a written justification
Populate metadata and verify all counts match before finalizing output.
